Full Cycle Talent Acquisition Support.overview: Provide end-to-end recruiting support to attract, assess, and secure qualified candidates while partnering closely with hiring managers.
guardrails: Do not limit the scope to one named position unless the agreement is explicitly intended to cover only that position.
The service must remain usable when roles are added, paused, closed, reopened, replaced, or changed during the contract term.
Keep it brief and executive-friendly: a title, one short paragraph, and 3 (occasionally 4) concise bullets — never a step-by-step account of the recruiting process.

Sourcing Support.overview: Provide focused candidate sourcing and pipeline-building support to help the Client identify strong prospects for priority roles.
guardrails: Do not automatically include full-cycle recruiting responsibilities (see exclusions). Only include them when specifically discussed and approved.
Keep it brief and executive-friendly: a title, one short paragraph, and 3 (occasionally 4) concise bullets.

HR Project Support.overview: Provide project-based HR consulting support tailored to the Client's priorities, with clear objectives and defined deliverables.
guardrails: Use a more specific project title (e.g. "HR Compliance Assessment") only when the discovery notes clearly describe a distinct, named project. Otherwise keep the title as "HR Project Support."
Do not insert unrelated HR services simply because they exist in the library.
Keep it brief and executive-friendly: a title, one short paragraph, and 3 (occasionally 4) concise bullets — never a full project plan.

Fractional/Interim HR Support.overview: Provide experienced interim HR leadership and hands-on support to maintain continuity while addressing immediate organizational priorities.
guardrails: Remain flexible enough to support changing HR priorities during the engagement.
Keep it brief and executive-friendly: a title, one short paragraph, and 3 (occasionally 4) concise bullets.

HR Subscription.overview: Provide ongoing HR guidance and support based on the organization's priorities, employee needs, and evolving business requirements.
guardrails: The scope may note that larger initiatives will be separately scoped and mutually approved.
Keep it brief and executive-friendly: a title, one short paragraph, and 3 (occasionally 4) concise bullets.
